chapter: Chapter 8: A Shirt in the Market

questions.question: Why do cotton farmers often depend on local traders or middlemen?

options.A: They lack access to direct markets

questions.explanation: Farmers usually lack transport, storage, and market access, so they rely on middlemen.

questions.question: Which sequence correctly represents the production chain of a shirt?
questions.explanation: Cotton is first spun into yarn, woven into fabric, stitched into shirts, and then sold.

questions.question: What is one major disadvantage for producers when middlemen dominate the market?
questions.explanation: Middlemen often take a share of profits, leaving producers with less income.

questions.question: Which stage of shirt production contributes most to adding design and style?
questions.explanation: During stitching, tailors cut, shape, and assemble the shirt according to design.

questions.question: Why is water management important in the textile industry?

options.A: Textile production uses large quantities of water
options.D: Water is only used in transport

questions.explanation: Water is heavily used in cotton farming, dyeing, and washing fabrics.

questions.question: Which factor mainly increases the final market price of a shirt?

options.A: Costs and profits added at each stage

questions.explanation: Each stage—spinning, weaving, stitching, trading—adds costs and profits.

questions.question: How do cooperatives help small producers in the market?
questions.explanation: Cooperatives allow producers to negotiate better prices and market access.

questions.question: Which stage transforms cotton fibers into threads suitable for weaving?
questions.explanation: Spinning converts raw cotton fibers into yarn.

questions.question: Why might consumers prefer ethically produced shirts?

options.A: They support fair wages and sustainable practices

questions.explanation: Ethical clothing supports fair labor conditions and environmental protection.

questions.question: Which process directly affects the environmental quality of nearby rivers?
questions.explanation: Chemical dyes released into water can pollute rivers and ecosystems.

questions.question: What economic concept describes the connection between all stages of shirt production?
questions.explanation: Supply chain refers to the sequence of production and distribution stages.

questions.question: Which worker group is most responsible for converting fabric into finished garments?
questions.explanation: Tailors cut and stitch cloth into garments like shirts.

questions.question: Why is cotton considered an important crop in textile production?

options.A: It provides the main raw material for fabrics
options.D: It is used only for decoration

questions.explanation: Cotton fibers are used to produce yarn and fabric for clothing.

questions.question: Which activity directly follows weaving in shirt production?
questions.explanation: After fabric is woven, it is cut and stitched into garments.

questions.question: Why are small producers often at a disadvantage in markets?
questions.explanation: Small producers usually lack market information and negotiating power.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes value addition?

options.A: Increasing product value at each production stage

questions.explanation: Each stage such as spinning, weaving, and stitching increases product value.

questions.question: How can sustainable clothing production benefit the environment?

options.A: By reducing pollution and conserving resources

questions.explanation: Sustainable practices reduce pollution and resource use.

questions.question: Which stage of shirt production requires artistic creativity?
questions.explanation: Designing and stitching require creativity to shape and style garments.

questions.question: Why might a cooperative reduce the role of middlemen?
questions.explanation: Cooperatives allow producers to sell products directly to markets.

questions.question: Which production stage most directly increases the durability of the shirt?
questions.explanation: Good stitching and finishing improve durability and quality.

questions.question: Why are supply chains important in modern markets?
questions.explanation: Supply chains connect all stages from production to consumers.

questions.question: Which group ensures shirts reach consumers in shops?
questions.explanation: Retailers sell finished goods to consumers.

questions.question: What is a possible environmental benefit of eco-friendly dyes?
questions.explanation: Eco-friendly dyes reduce harmful chemicals entering water bodies.

questions.question: Which production stage connects agriculture to textile manufacturing?
questions.explanation: Spinning converts agricultural cotton into industrial yarn.

questions.question: Why is understanding production chains useful for consumers?

options.A: It helps them make informed and responsible choices
options.B: It eliminates the need to buy clothes

questions.explanation: Awareness of production processes helps consumers support ethical and sustainable products.
